titleStmt.title: SII 29.53: original edition by P. N. Narasimha Murthy
No. 53. (A.R. No. 69 of 1961-62.) KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.

summary.p: On broken pieces kept in the Museum Acc. No. 86-87.

It records the gift of a village (name lost) included in Koṁḍada-12 for worship and offerings to god Sōmēśvara by the chief Tonḍaya Chōḍa-mahārāja of Kandūru, who was ruling over Kollipāke-7000. Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla is stated to have been ruling over the empire from the capital Kalyāṇa. Kandeṇa was the composer of this record.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 16, Prajāpati, solar eclipse. The intended tithi may correspond to 1091 A.D., May 21 [Wednesday] f.d.t. 18. The only solar eclipse of the year occured on this day. The corresponding month and tithi were Vaiśākha, amāvāsye.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti samasta bhuvanāśraya
śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārā
jādhirāja paramēśvara pa
rama bhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśra
ya kuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyā
bharaṇa śrīmatribhuva
namalladēvara vijaya
rājya muttarōttarābhivṛ

ddhi pravarddhamāna mācaṁ
drārka tāraṁbaraṁ kalyā
ṇada nelevīḍinoḷsukha saṁ
katāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyvu
ttumire tatpāda padmōpajī
vi samadhigata paṁca mahā
sa(śa)bda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ
vīra mahēśvaraṁ kōḷyu
rppuravarēśvaraṁ sūryyavaṁ
sō(śō)dbhava kuḷatiḷakaṁ kāsya
pa gōtra karikālānvaya
nāmādi samasta praśasti

sahitaṁ śrī manmahāmaṇḍaḷē
śvaraṁ kandūru toṇḍaya cōḍa
mahārājaṁ kōḷipāka Ē
ḷsāsira mumasukhadināḷutta
viḻdu śrīmacchāḷukya vikra
magālada hadināṟaneya pra
jāpati saṁvatsarada sūryya
grahaṇa nimittadiṁ koṁḍada pa

nneraḍaṟa baḷiya
bāḷa śrī sōmēśvaradēvargge Aṁ
ga bhōgakkaṁ raṁga bhōgakkaṁ sa
rvvanamasyamāge koṭṭaṁ . ©
sāmānyōyaṁ dharmasētu
nripāṇāṁ kālē kālē pāla
niyō bhavadbhiḥ sarvā nētān bhā
vinaḥ pārtthivēṁdrān bhūyō
bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ
svadatta paradatta vāyō harē
ti vasundharā saṣṭirvvarśa(ṣa) saha
srāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ
lipitō kandeṇa .
Digital edition of SII 29.53 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
